canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, VSR is consistently used as a benchmark to measure the construct of spatial reasoning. It is defined as a "closed-set benchmark that tests a model's understanding of coarse object spatial relationships" (EMMA: Empowering Multi-modal Mamba with Structural and Hierarchical Alignment). This specific focus is reinforced by source material noting that VSR "tests coarse object spatial relationships." The relationship between the instrument and the construct is its most prevalent feature in the data, with multiple papers employing VSR to evaluate spatial reasoning. The available information presents a uniform characterization of VSR as a measurement tool for this particular capability.
papers: EMMA: Empowering Multi-modal Mamba with Structural and Hierarchical Alignment
Re-Imagining Multimodal Instruction Tuning: A Representation View
